frameworks.name: hierarchical Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: feature estimate marginalized or conditioned over discrete high-level interpretations

frameworks.name: categorical perception
frameworks.one_line: category-based perception treated as a hierarchical inference task

frameworks.name: causal cue combination
frameworks.one_line: cue integration cast as inference over a causal latent

frameworks.name: full marginalization (model averaging)
frameworks.one_line: baseline that averages estimates across all high-level interpretations

core_question: What is the effect on feature-level estimation accuracy of committing to a single high-level interpretation rather than considering all alternatives?
key_assumptions: feature estimation can be conditioned on a single discrete high-level interpretation
category-based perception and causal cue combination share a common hierarchical inference structure
estimation accuracy is the relevant criterion for evaluating commitment
manipulations: commitment strategy: single high-level interpretation vs full marginalization
feature value relative to decision boundaries between high-level interpretations
task type: category-based perception vs causal cue combination
key_findings: overall estimation accuracy is reduced when conditioning on a single high-level interpretation
the reduction is non-uniform across the feature range
near decision boundaries commitment is worse than full inference
away from decision boundaries commitment is better than full inference
interpretations: commitment to a single interpretation yields local accuracy benefits that can rationalize the strategy
the apparent short-sightedness of committing is not uniformly irrational at the feature level
limitations_noted: analysis restricted to two specific hierarchical inference tasks
evaluation based on model simulations rather than direct empirical test
